title: Trump looms large as Nato grapples with challenge of rearming Europe
description: The US president's inflammatory words have punctuated the image of unity at this crucial gathering, Security Correspondent Frank Gardner writes.
content:
Black-clad Turkish police on street corners, giant blue banners proclaiming "The Key to Peace" and a ceremonial welcome for Donald Trump.
This may be a Nato summit but it's the US president who's had the metaphorical red carpet laid out for him.

title: Revealed: Farage’s £5m gift reported to UK crime agency over money laundering concerns
description: Exclusive: Latest Guardian revelation about gift from cryptocurrency tycoon comes as Reform UK leader forces byelection
content:
The £5m gift to Nigel Farage by a cryptocurrency billionaire was reported to the National Crime Agency by bankers who were concerned it may have been laundered money, the Guardian can reveal.
The disclosure will put further pressure on the Reform UK ... [6123 chars]

title: Marine Le Pen to run for French presidency and appeal conviction in top court
description: The hard-right National Rally leader has ended months of speculation, after a court ruled she could run but would have to wear a tag for a year.
content:
French hard-right leader Marine Le Pen has said she will run for the presidency in 2027, and appeal to the country's highest court against an embezzlement conviction that requires her to wear an electronic tag for a year.
"The campaign begins tonight... [4182 chars]

title: World Cup 2026: Jordan Henderson injury to prompt hoarding advice for England players
description: England players will be advised not to jump over advertising hoardings after Jordan Henderson suffered a freak injury.
content:
England players will be advised not to jump over advertising hoardings after Jordan Henderson suffered a freak injury in the aftermath of their World Cup victory over Mexico.

title: ‘Serious consequences’: Farage attacks Sky News after question about George Cottrell
description: Reform UK leader confronts journalist after question about gifts from friend and convicted fraudster
content:
Nigel Farage has said Sky News bosses face “serious consequences” in an outburst triggered by questions over fresh revelations about his finances.
